message:
chore(beads): update ft-1memj.15 with window crate fail-closed progress slice

Update issue ft-1memj.15 ([async] Migrate vendored window crate event
loop to asupersync) with RusticCondor's March 22 progress comment
documenting the fail-closed window-op futures work:

- Updated timestamp from 2026-03-22T23:50:16Z to 2026-03-23T00:16:30Z
- Added comment #2471 describing the fail-closed window-op promise
  helpers, platform shim updates (macOS/Wayland/X11/Windows), clipboard
  error propagation, validation results (rustfmt pass, ubs scan), and
  coordination notes about agent mail transient issues

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -340,7 +340,7 @@
 {"id":"ft-1memj.12","title":"[gui][gpu] Dirty-rect rendering for split pane efficiency","description":"# Dirty-rect rendering for split pane efficiency

## What
Only re-render terminal cells that have actually changed, using the vendored term's DirtyRect infrastructure.

## Background
The vendored frankenterm/term already has:
- `DirtyRect` type for tile-based dirty region tracking
- `last_good_frame` snapshot for resize artifact recovery

WezTerm's stock renderer redraws the entire screen every frame. With 50+ agent panes in splits, this means rendering 50 pane surfaces when only 1 pane has new output.

## How
1. Each pane maintains a dirty region (set of changed cell coordinates)
2. On render: only update GPU vertex buffer for dirty cells
3. Use the DirtyRect data from the vendored term layer
4. Implement per-pane render targets (texture or framebuffer region)
5. Composite pane textures into the final frame only when a pane is dirty

## Performance Target
With 50 split panes where only 5 have active output:
- Old: 50 full-surface redraws per frame
- New: 5 partial updates (dirty rects only) + composite

Expected speedup: 5-10x reduction in GPU work for typical agent swarm layouts.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Only dirty cells are re-rendered (verified via GPU instrumentation)
- 50+ split panes at 60fps with < 10% GPU utilization on M1
- No visual artifacts from partial updates
- Resize falls back to full redraw (acceptable)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":360,"created_at":"2026-03-02T06:56:49.382264Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-02T18:44:47.593337Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["gpu","gui","performance"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-1memj.12","depends_on_id":"ft-1memj","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-02T06:56:49.382264Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-1memj.12","depends_on_id":"ft-1memj.11","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T06:59:26.793451Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-1memj.12","depends_on_id":"ft-2oph2","type":"related","created_at":"2026-03-02T07:00:04.486016Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1699,"issue_id":"ft-1memj.12","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"CORRECTION: The vendored term crate does NOT have a named DirtyRect type. Dirty tracking is implemented via SequenceNo on individual Lines (each Line has a seq number that increments on modification). The renderer should compare per-line SequenceNo against the last-rendered seq to determine which lines are dirty. The description references 'DirtyRect data from the vendored term layer' — this should be restated as 'SequenceNo-based line-level dirty tracking from the vendored term layer'. The overall approach (only re-render changed cells) is correct but the mechanism is line-sequence-based, not tile-rect-based. This actually simplifies the implementation since lines are the natural unit for terminal rendering.","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:44:47Z"}]}
 {"id":"ft-1memj.13","title":"[async][epic] Profound asupersync Integration — Zero Tokio","description":"# Profound asupersync Integration — Zero Tokio

## What
Complete the migration from tokio to asupersync across the entire FrankenTerm stack, including frankenterm-gui, all vendored crates, and all feature-gated modules. The end state is ZERO tokio/smol/async-io imports anywhere in the dependency tree.

## Background
Multiple existing beads track pieces of this (ft-e34d9, ft-brc7d, ft-2h1ha, etc.) but the work is fragmented. This bead provides the comprehensive scope for the GUI integration specifically.

The key insight: frankenterm-gui will be the largest consumer of async I/O (mux protocol, pty I/O, font loading, network connections). If the GUI uses tokio while everything else uses asupersync, we get runtime conflicts and unnecessary bloat.

## Current State
- `runtime_compat.rs` provides a thin compatibility layer (CompatRuntime) that wraps either tokio or asupersync
- Many callsites still use tokio types directly (tokio::spawn, tokio::sync::mpsc, etc.)
- Vendored crates (mux, ssh, pty) use smol/async-io internally
- FastMCP and FastAPI frameworks depend on tokio

## Scope for GUI Integration
1. frankenterm-gui must boot on asupersync runtime (not tokio)
2. Mux client I/O (Unix socket) must use asupersync
3. PTY I/O must use asupersync (the pty crate uses polling/async-io)
4. Font loading (async file I/O) must use asupersync
5. Network connections (SSH, TLS for distributed) must use asupersync
6. All vendored crates must compile without tokio/smol/async-io

## Relationship to Existing Beads
This bead coordinates with but does not replace:
- ft-e34d9 (the broad runtime migration epic)
- ft-brc7d (vendored crate migration)
- ft-2h1ha (tokio removal)
- ft-e34d9.10.8.1 (runtime eradication)

This bead focuses on the GUI-specific aspects and the final integration testing.

## Acceptance Criteria
- `cargo tree -p frankenterm-gui | grep tokio` returns nothing
- `cargo tree -p frankenterm-gui | grep smol` returns nothing
- `cargo tree -p frankenterm-gui | grep async-io` returns nothing
- frankenterm-gui runs on asupersync CompatRuntime
- All async operations (pty, mux, network, file I/O) use asupersync primitives
- No performance regression vs tokio baseline (latency benchmarks)","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"feature","estimated_minutes":4800,"created_at":"2026-03-02T06:57:27.310800Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-02T18:33:54.413212Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["asupersync","epic","runtime"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-1memj.13","depends_on_id":"ft-1memj","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-02T06:57:27.310800Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-1memj.13","depends_on_id":"ft-1memj.14","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T06:59:27.984406Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-1memj.13","depends_on_id":"ft-1memj.15","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T06:59:28.279372Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-1memj.13","depends_on_id":"ft-2h1ha","type":"related","created_at":"2026-03-02T07:00:03.590743Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-1memj.13","depends_on_id":"ft-brc7d","type":"related","created_at":"2026-03-02T07:00:03.291093Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-1memj.13","depends_on_id":"ft-e34d9","type":"related","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:32:09.452244Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1690,"issue_id":"ft-1memj.13","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Review: Changed ft-e34d9 from blocks to related. The GUI asupersync migration (.13) should proceed in parallel with the broader runtime migration epic, not be gated on it.","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:33:54Z"}]}
 {"id":"ft-1memj.14","title":"[async] Migrate vendored pty crate from async-io to asupersync","description":"# Migrate vendored pty crate from async-io/smol to asupersync

## What
Replace smol/async-io in the ALREADY VENDORED frankenterm/pty crate with asupersync I/O primitives.

## Key Fact
The pty crate is ALREADY in the workspace at `frankenterm/pty/` (unlike window/font which need vendoring). This task can start immediately with no blockers.

## Current Dependencies
- `smol.workspace = true` — used for async PTY I/O
- `filedescriptor.workspace = true` — raw fd management (keep this, it's not async-runtime-specific)

## Why
The PTY crate is the lowest-level I/O layer — it reads/writes bytes to the pseudoterminal device. If this uses smol/async-io, every consumer (term, mux, GUI) needs a smol runtime available, or bridges at every boundary. asupersync unification eliminates this.

## How
1. Replace `smol::Async<OwnedFd>` wrappers with asupersync's async fd wrapper
2. Replace smol channel usage with asupersync channels
3. Update `async_ossl` integration (it has `async-io` feature flag: `async_ossl/async-io`)
4. Ensure child process spawning (fork/exec) works correctly with asupersync's reactor
5. Test: open 100 PTYs concurrently, verify no fd leaks, verify SIGCHLD delivery

## Unit Tests Required
- `test_pty_read_write_basic` — open pty, write "echo hello", read output
- `test_pty_concurrent_100` — 100 concurrent PTY sessions, no fd leak
- `test_pty_sigchld_delivery` — child exit produces correct WaitStatus
- `test_pty_resize_sigwinch` — SIGWINCH propagates on resize
- `test_pty_large_output` — 10 MB output stream without deadlock
- `test_pty_async_cancellation` — drop future mid-read, no resource leak

## Acceptance Criteria
- `cargo tree -p pty | grep -E "smol|async-io"` returns nothing
- PTY read/write latency unchanged (< 1ms for small writes)
- 100 concurrent PTY sessions work without fd exhaustion
- Signal handling (SIGCHLD, SIGWINCH) works correctly
- All unit tests pass","notes":"Completed migration in vendored pty crate:
- frankenterm/pty/Cargo.toml dev-dependency switched from smol/futures to asupersync.
- frankenterm/pty/examples/whoami_async.rs migrated from smol::block_on/unblock/BufReader usage to asupersync runtime + spawn_blocking.
- frankenterm/pty/src/lib.rs added migration guard tests to prevent reintroducing smol/async_io tokens in manifest/source/examples.
Validation evidence:
1) rch exec -- sh -lc 'cargo tree -p portable-pty -e normal,build,dev | grep -E "smol|async-io"' => exit 1 (no matches).
2) rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/indigokite-pty-check cargo check -p portable-pty --examples => Finished dev profile successfully.
3) rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/indigokite-pty-test cargo test -p portable-pty --lib => 106 passed, 0 failed.
4) rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/indigokite-pty-clippy cargo clippy -p portable-pty --all-targets -- -D warnings => Finished without warnings.
Note: rch selected worker vmi1153651 repeatedly failed SSH and fell back to local execution automatically; commands were still invoked through rch per policy.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"PinkHill","estimated_minutes":360,"created_at":"2026-03-02T06:57:27.627126Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-02T18:53:03.494576Z","closed_at":"2026-03-02T18:53:03.494555Z","close_reason":"Completed smol/async-io migration for vendored pty with validation evidence","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["asupersync","pty","vendored"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-1memj.14","depends_on_id":"ft-1memj","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-02T06:57:27.627126Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1698,"issue_id":"ft-1memj.14","author":"PinkHill","text":"Slice update (PinkHill): removed portable-pty dev deps on smol/futures; added asupersync dev dependency; rewrote frankenterm/pty/examples/whoami_async.rs from smol::block_on/unblock/Unblock to asupersync RuntimeBuilder + spawn_blocking; added migration guard tests in pty/src/lib.rs to fail if smol/async_io tokens reappear in manifest/source/examples. Validation: rch exec cargo tree -p portable-pty shows no smol/async-io; rch exec cargo check -p portable-pty --all-targets passed; rch exec cargo test -p portable-pty --lib passed (106 tests). Workspace-wide check/clippy/fmt gates are currently being run separately and encountering substantial pre-existing workspace churn unrelated to this slice.","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:44:34Z"}]}
-{"id":"ft-1memj.15","title":"[async] Migrate vendored window crate event loop to asupersync","description":"# Migrate vendored window crate event loop to asupersync

## What
Replace the window crate's event loop (currently based on raw platform APIs + smol integration) with asupersync.

## Why
The window crate's event loop is the GUI's main loop — it drives input handling, rendering, and platform events. If this runs on a different async runtime than the rest of FrankenTerm, we need bridges at every boundary.

## Current Implementation
The window crate uses:
- macOS: Cocoa NSApplication run loop with async callbacks
- Linux/X11: xcb event loop
- Linux/Wayland: wayland-client event loop
- All platforms: smol-based async tasks for I/O within the event loop

## How
1. Replace smol task spawning within the event loop with asupersync::spawn
2. Replace smol channels with asupersync channels for GUI↔async communication
3. Ensure platform event loops integrate with asupersync's reactor (register platform fd as event source)
4. macOS-specific: NSApplication.run() is blocking — need to interleave with asupersync via RunLoop integration

## Complexity
This is one of the hardest migration tasks because the window event loop is deeply intertwined with platform APIs. The macOS Cocoa integration uses @autoreleasepool and NSRunLoop which have specific threading requirements.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Window event loop runs on asupersync (no smol tasks)
- Input latency unchanged (< 2ms from keypress to terminal echo)
- Window management (resize, move, minimize, fullscreen) works
- macOS-specific: Cocoa integration stable (no autorelease pool crashes)","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":480,"created_at":"2026-03-02T06:57:27.940109Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-22T23:50:16.698549Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["asupersync","gui","vendored","window"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-1memj.15","depends_on_id":"ft-1memj","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-02T06:57:27.940109Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-1memj.15","depends_on_id":"ft-1memj.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T06:59:27.684576Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-1memj.15","depends_on_id":"ft-brc7d","type":"related","created_at":"2026-03-02T19:11:14.198076Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1734,"issue_id":"ft-1memj.15","author":"PurpleRiver","text":"Progress slice (PurpleRiver, 2026-03-02): bounded discovery + timer-abstraction validation for `ft-1memj.15`.

Findings:
- Event-loop timer call-sites in vendored `window` are already routed through `promise::spawn::sleep(...)` in current HEAD across:
  - `frankenterm/window/src/os/macos/window.rs`
  - `frankenterm/window/src/os/x11/window.rs`
  - `frankenterm/window/src/os/windows/window.rs`
  - `frankenterm/window/src/os/wayland/window.rs`
  - `frankenterm/window/src/os/xdg_desktop_portal.rs`
- Net: no additional production timer-callsite edits were needed in this slice.

Code changes landed:
- `frankenterm/promise/src/spawn.rs`
  - added focused tests for the timer helper API:
    - `sleep_waits_at_least_requested_duration`
    - `sleep_zero_duration_completes`

Validation (heavy commands via `rch exec`):
1) `cargo check -p window --all-targets` => PASS (warnings only, primarily existing macro/cfg warnings in vendored GUI crates).
2) `cargo test -p promise --lib sleep_ -- --nocapture` => PASS (2 passed).

Notes:
- This slice confirms timer usage abstraction is in place and test-covered.
- Remaining hard migration work for this bead is deeper scheduler/runtime plumbing in `promise::spawn` and platform event-loop integration semantics (main-thread dispatch + structured concurrency), not raw timer callsites.
","created_at":"2026-03-02T19:58:18Z"},{"id":2355,"issue_id":"ft-1memj.15","author":"TealMouse","text":"Starting a bounded March 14 slice on ft-1memj.15: switch the centralized promise timer helper in frankenterm/promise/src/spawn.rs from unconditional async-io to feature-gated asupersync wall-clock sleep under async-asupersync. This stays within the existing timer abstraction validated earlier, avoids broad GUI/event-loop churn, and should improve runtime alignment for vendored window timer call-sites that already route through promise::spawn::sleep().","created_at":"2026-03-14T21:43:36Z"}]}
+{"id":"ft-1memj.15","title":"[async] Migrate vendored window crate event loop to asupersync","description":"# Migrate vendored window crate event loop to asupersync

## What
Replace the window crate's event loop (currently based on raw platform APIs + smol integration) with asupersync.

## Why
The window crate's event loop is the GUI's main loop — it drives input handling, rendering, and platform events. If this runs on a different async runtime than the rest of FrankenTerm, we need bridges at every boundary.

## Current Implementation
The window crate uses:
- macOS: Cocoa NSApplication run loop with async callbacks
- Linux/X11: xcb event loop
- Linux/Wayland: wayland-client event loop
- All platforms: smol-based async tasks for I/O within the event loop

## How
1. Replace smol task spawning within the event loop with asupersync::spawn
2. Replace smol channels with asupersync channels for GUI↔async communication
3. Ensure platform event loops integrate with asupersync's reactor (register platform fd as event source)
4. macOS-specific: NSApplication.run() is blocking — need to interleave with asupersync via RunLoop integration

## Complexity
This is one of the hardest migration tasks because the window event loop is deeply intertwined with platform APIs. The macOS Cocoa integration uses @autoreleasepool and NSRunLoop which have specific threading requirements.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Window event loop runs on asupersync (no smol tasks)
- Input latency unchanged (< 2ms from keypress to terminal echo)
- Window management (resize, move, minimize, fullscreen) works
- macOS-specific: Cocoa integration stable (no autorelease pool crashes)","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":480,"created_at":"2026-03-02T06:57:27.940109Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-23T00:16:30.794580Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["asupersync","gui","vendored","window"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-1memj.15","depends_on_id":"ft-1memj","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-02T06:57:27.940109Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-1memj.15","depends_on_id":"ft-1memj.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T06:59:27.684576Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-1memj.15","depends_on_id":"ft-brc7d","type":"related","created_at":"2026-03-02T19:11:14.198076Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1734,"issue_id":"ft-1memj.15","author":"PurpleRiver","text":"Progress slice (PurpleRiver, 2026-03-02): bounded discovery + timer-abstraction validation for `ft-1memj.15`.

Findings:
- Event-loop timer call-sites in vendored `window` are already routed through `promise::spawn::sleep(...)` in current HEAD across:
  - `frankenterm/window/src/os/macos/window.rs`
  - `frankenterm/window/src/os/x11/window.rs`
  - `frankenterm/window/src/os/windows/window.rs`
  - `frankenterm/window/src/os/wayland/window.rs`
  - `frankenterm/window/src/os/xdg_desktop_portal.rs`
- Net: no additional production timer-callsite edits were needed in this slice.

Code changes landed:
- `frankenterm/promise/src/spawn.rs`
  - added focused tests for the timer helper API:
    - `sleep_waits_at_least_requested_duration`
    - `sleep_zero_duration_completes`

Validation (heavy commands via `rch exec`):
1) `cargo check -p window --all-targets` => PASS (warnings only, primarily existing macro/cfg warnings in vendored GUI crates).
2) `cargo test -p promise --lib sleep_ -- --nocapture` => PASS (2 passed).

Notes:
- This slice confirms timer usage abstraction is in place and test-covered.
- Remaining hard migration work for this bead is deeper scheduler/runtime plumbing in `promise::spawn` and platform event-loop integration semantics (main-thread dispatch + structured concurrency), not raw timer callsites.
","created_at":"2026-03-02T19:58:18Z"},{"id":2355,"issue_id":"ft-1memj.15","author":"TealMouse","text":"Starting a bounded March 14 slice on ft-1memj.15: switch the centralized promise timer helper in frankenterm/promise/src/spawn.rs from unconditional async-io to feature-gated asupersync wall-clock sleep under async-asupersync. This stays within the existing timer abstraction validated earlier, avoids broad GUI/event-loop churn, and should improve runtime alignment for vendored window timer call-sites that already route through promise::spawn::sleep().","created_at":"2026-03-14T21:43:36Z"},{"id":2471,"issue_id":"ft-1memj.15","author":"RusticCondor","text":"Progress slice (RusticCondor, 2026-03-22): fail-closed window-op futures + clipboard scheduling error propagation in vendored window crate.

Changes in working tree:
- Added shared helpers in frankenterm/window/src/connection.rs to create window-op promises and fail them closed when a target window has already been destroyed.
- Updated with_window_inner shims in frankenterm/window/src/os/wayland/connection.rs, frankenterm/window/src/os/x11/connection.rs, frankenterm/window/src/os/macos/connection.rs, and frankenterm/window/src/os/windows/connection.rs so returned futures resolve to an error instead of remaining pending forever on missing windows.
- Updated clipboard request paths in frankenterm/window/src/os/wayland/window.rs and frankenterm/window/src/os/x11/window.rs to propagate those scheduling errors into the outer clipboard future instead of hanging.
- Added a focused unit test in frankenterm/window/src/connection.rs for the fail-closed helper behavior.

Validation:
- rustfmt --edition 2024 --check on touched window files: PASS.
- git diff --check on touched files: PASS.
- ubs --diff --only=rust,toml . completed, but the emitted critical/warning volume is dominated by broader repo-wide modified-file inventory and one pre-existing resource-lifecycle warning on the existing detached std::thread::spawn in wayland clipboard reading; no scanner finding specifically contradicted this fail-closed change.

Heavy validation status:
- Remote cargo validation for the window crate remains blocked on the Linux rch worker image missing GUI system packages (x11.pc / wayland-client.pc), so no remote cargo check/test result for this slice yet.

Coordination:
- Agent Mail fetch works intermittently, but ack/respond/send contact operations are currently timing out or returning transient DB errors, so coordination updates are being mirrored here in beads comments.","created_at":"2026-03-23T00:16:30Z"}]}
 {"id":"ft-1memj.16","title":"[gui][bundle] Update FrankenTerm.app to use custom GUI binary","description":"# Update FrankenTerm.app to use custom GUI binary

## What
Update scripts/create-macos-bundle.sh to build and bundle frankenterm-gui from source instead of copying stock wezterm-gui from /Applications/WezTerm.app.

## Current State
The bundle script copies:
- /Applications/WezTerm.app/Contents/MacOS/wezterm-gui (135 MB stock binary)
- /Applications/WezTerm.app/Contents/MacOS/wezterm (67 MB CLI)
- /Applications/WezTerm.app/Contents/MacOS/wezterm-mux-server (62 MB)

## Target State
The bundle script builds:
- crates/frankenterm-gui → Contents/MacOS/frankenterm-gui (custom binary)
- crates/frankenterm → Contents/MacOS/ft (management CLI)
- (optional) wezterm-mux-server from vendored crates

## How
1. Update create-macos-bundle.sh:
   - `cargo build --release --bin frankenterm-gui --bin ft`
   - Copy from target/release/ instead of /Applications/WezTerm.app/
   - No more --wezterm-app flag
2. Update Info.plist CFBundleExecutable from "wezterm-gui" to "frankenterm-gui"
3. Embed icon directly in the binary via build.rs (optional, macOS resource fork)
4. Update CI release workflow to build the .app bundle

## Acceptance Criteria
- `./scripts/create-macos-bundle.sh` builds everything from source
- FrankenTerm.app launches frankenterm-gui (custom binary)
- No dependency on a pre-installed WezTerm.app
- App shows "FrankenTerm" in Activity Monitor with correct icon","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":120,"created_at":"2026-03-02T06:58:19.029316Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-02T22:42:25.813047Z","closed_at":"2026-03-02T22:42:25.813033Z","close_reason":"Bundle script builds from source (frankenterm-gui + ft). No WezTerm.app dependency. Info.plist points to frankenterm-gui. Default config bundled in Resources.  {"id":"ft-1memj.17","title":"[gui][swarm] Agent-aware session management in GUI","description":"# Agent-aware session management in GUI

## What
Add agent-fleet-aware features directly in the GUI that go beyond what WezTerm provides.

## Why
WezTerm was designed for human users. FrankenTerm needs to handle 200+ AI agent sessions simultaneously. The GUI itself needs to be aware of agent workloads.

## Features
1. **Agent pane identification**: Visual indicators showing which panes are controlled by AI agents vs human-interactive
   - Color-coded tab/pane borders (green=active agent, yellow=thinking, red=stuck, gray=idle)
   - Agent name overlay in pane title bar

2. **Backpressure visualization**: Show backpressure tier directly in the pane chrome
   - Green/Yellow/Red/Black tier indicator
   - Queue depth sparkline
   - Rate limit status per pane

3. **Mass pane operations**: GUI shortcuts for agent swarm management
   - "Kill all stuck agents" (Cmd+Shift+K)
   - "Pause all agent input" (Cmd+Shift+P)
   - "Focus on error panes" (Cmd+Shift+E) — filter view to panes with detected errors

4. **Smart pane auto-layout**: When agents create many panes, auto-arrange them by:
   - Domain (group by project)
   - Status (errors first, active next, idle last)
   - Activity (most recent output on top)

5. **Resource monitoring overlay**: Show system resource usage (CPU, memory, disk) per agent pane group

## Integration Points
- Reads from ft watch daemon's health data (backpressure tiers, agent status)
- Uses the mux's CollapsePriority to auto-collapse idle agent panes
- Leverages swap layouts for dynamic rearrangement

## Acceptance Criteria
- Agent panes visually distinguished from human panes
- Backpressure tier visible in pane chrome
- Mass operations work on all agent panes simultaneously
- Auto-layout arranges 50+ panes coherently","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":360,"created_at":"2026-03-02T06:58:19.377922Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-03T00:27:04.779515Z","closed_at":"2026-03-03T00:27:04.779498Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["agents","gui","swarm"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-1memj.17","depends_on_id":"ft-1i2ge","type":"related","created_at":"2026-03-02T19:08:33.759578Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-1memj.17","depends_on_id":"ft-1memj","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-02T06:58:19.377922Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-1memj.17","depends_on_id":"ft-1memj.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T06:59:29.177528Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-1memj.17","depends_on_id":"ft-1memj.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T06:59:29.481099Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-1memj.17","depends_on_id":"ft-1memj.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:39:37.296624Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-1memj.17","depends_on_id":"ft-iehgn","type":"related","created_at":"2026-03-02T07:00:04.187608Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1687,"issue_id":"ft-1memj.17","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"## Test Coverage (implemented in .30)

Agent swarm stress tests:
- 50/100/200 pane scale tests (memory, CPU, frame time)
- Concurrent output throughput
- Backpressure activation under load
- Mass pane operations (kill stuck, pause all, focus errors)

Tests are tracked in ft-1memj.30 (Agent swarm stress test suite).","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:25:13Z"},{"id":1697,"issue_id":"ft-1memj.17","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Review: Consider adding macOS native notifications (UNUserNotificationCenter) for critical agent events when GUI is in background. When an agent hits a rate limit or error, the operator needs to know even if the terminal is behind other windows. This is a small addition to the agent-aware session management but high user impact.","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:40:28Z"},{"id":1707,"issue_id":"ft-1memj.17","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"GAP: The bead defines visual states (green=active, yellow=thinking, red=stuck, gray=idle) but does NOT specify the detection logic for each state. This must be defined:
- ACTIVE (green): pane produced output in last 5 seconds
- THINKING (yellow): pane has received input but produced no output for 5-30 seconds (agent is processing)
- STUCK (red): pane has produced no output for >30 seconds after receiving input, OR watchdog/circuit breaker has flagged it, OR pattern engine detected a rate limit/error
- IDLE (gray): pane has received no input AND produced no output for >60 seconds
These thresholds should be configurable in frankenterm.toml under [agent_detection]. The detection should leverage existing frankenterm-core infrastructure: watchdog (src/watchdog.rs), circuit_breaker (src/circuit_breaker.rs), and pattern engine events.","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:59:19Z"},{"id":1749,"issue_id":"ft-1memj.17","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Claimed by DustyFinch. Starting implementation of agent-aware session management GUI features.","created_at":"2026-03-03T00:14:52Z"}]}
 {"id":"ft-1memj.18","title":"[gui][swarm] Integrated TUI dashboard within GUI (not separate ft tui)","description":"# Integrated TUI dashboard within GUI (not separate ft tui)

## What
Embed the TUI dashboard (currently `ft tui`, a separate terminal app) as a built-in panel/tab within frankenterm-gui itself.

## Why
Currently the dashboard runs as a separate terminal process (`ft tui`) that the user opens in a terminal. This is circular — you need a terminal to view the terminal management dashboard. The dashboard should be a native panel within the GUI.

## How
1. Add a "Dashboard" tab type to frankenterm-gui (alongside regular terminal tabs)
2. Render the dashboard content using the GPU renderer (not via a PTY)
3. Dashboard reads from the same data sources as `ft tui`:
   - SQLite storage for events, pane history, search
   - Live pane state from mux
   - Health data from watchdog/backpressure/circuit breaker
4. Allow toggling dashboard with a keybinding (e.g., Cmd+Shift+D)
5. Dashboard should be a side panel or overlay, not replacing the terminal view

## Design Inspiration
- Zellij's built-in status bar and session manager
- tmux's built-in status line and chooser
- iTerm2's "Toolbelt" side panel

## Acceptance Criteria
- Dashboard accessible via keybinding without leaving the GUI
- Shows: pane list, health, events, search — same as ft tui
- Rendered natively (not via a PTY running ft tui)
- Can interact with dashboard while terminals remain visible","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":240,"created_at":"2026-03-02T06:58:19.725007Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-03T00:33:20.650346Z","closed_at":"2026-03-03T00:33:20.650326Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["dashboard","gui","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-1memj.18","depends_on_id":"ft-1memj","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-02T06:58:19.725007Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-1memj.18","depends_on_id":"ft-1memj.17","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T06:59:29.782517Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1710,"issue_id":"ft-1memj.18","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"UX REQUIREMENT: The embedded dashboard's search results must be clickable/navigable — selecting a search result should focus the corresponding pane and scroll to the matched content. This is the 'search → jump to pane → see context' workflow that makes FrankenTerm dramatically better than running ft search in a separate terminal. Implementation: search results include pane_id and approximate line number; clicking a result sends a FocusPane(pane_id) + ScrollToLine(line) command to the mux.","created_at":"2026-03-02T19:00:20Z"},{"id":1756,"issue_id":"ft-1memj.18","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Claimed by DustyFinch. Building on ft-1memj.17 agent state types for dashboard integration.","created_at":"2026-03-03T00:30:05Z"}]}
